---
# EDIT AT YOUR OWN PERIL #
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    comments:
      hypothesis: true
    citeproc: true
    csl: journal-of-the-acm.csl
  allegheny-cis-pdf: default

natbiboptions: numbers
# EDIT AT YOUR OWN PERIL #

title: "WorkoutEvaluator: An iOS App That Provides Personalized Exercise Feedback Through User-provided Data"


firstreader: Gregory Kapfhammer 
secondreader: Oliver Bonham-Carter

bibliography: references.bib

author:
  - name: Evan Nelson
    affiliation:
      name: Allegheny College
      address: 520 N. Main St., Box Q
      city: Meadville
      state: Pennsylvania
      country: USA
      postal-code: 16335

abstract: |
  This project presents WorkoutEvaluator, a fully developed iOS application designed to enhance strength training through structured workout tracking, intuitive progress visualization, and personalized performance feedback based on user-entered data. The motivation for this work arises from limitations in existing fitness applications, which often depend on wearable devices or fail to provide meaningful, individualized insights for resistance training. In contrast, this application emphasizes simplicity, accessibility, and actionable guidance tailored to each user’s progress. The system includes key features such as exercise logging, dynamic progress charts, evaluation metrics that highlight trends and improvements, achievement-based gamification to increase motivation, and a streamlined exercise selection experience. To assess performance and scalability, controlled experiments were conducted using datasets ranging from 100 to 10,000 workout entries, measuring load times, query efficiency, and interface responsiveness. Results demonstrate that the application remains fast, responsive, and reliable even at larger scales, supporting its effectiveness for consistent, long-term use. Overall, this work delivers a practical and engaging fitness tool that not only records workouts but actively helps users understand their progress, stay motivated, and improve their training outcomes, positioning it as a compelling alternative to existing solutions in the health and fitness app space.
---

{{< include _introduction.qmd >}}

{{< include _related_work.qmd >}}

{{< include _methods.qmd >}}

{{< include _experiments.qmd >}}

{{< include _conclusion.qmd >}}
